choices.text: 3,000-5,000 TPS typical, 400ms blocks, <$0.001 fees. Theoretical 65k TPS. Trade-offs: high validator hardware reqs, occasional chain halts.
Cryptographic clock via continuous hash chain. Validators don't coordinate on time; chain self-orders. Enables parallel block production = throughput advantage.
Address (32-byte pubkey), owner (controlling program), lamports (SOL), data (serialized bytes), executable. All state in accounts; programs read/write.
Address Lookup Tables (ALT) + V0 Versioned Transactions. ALT stores accounts on-chain; tx references by 1-byte index. Fits within 1,232 byte limit. Standard for DEX routing + DeFi.
Solana CLI + Rust + Anchor + Helius/QuickNode RPC. anchor init scaffolds; anchor build compiles to BPF; anchor deploy ships. Local validator (solana-test-validator) for fast iteration.

questions.explanation: CLI + Rust + Anchor + paid RPC = standard stack.
